name: Athenian Chicken Rolls
recipeInstructions:
1. Place chicken between two pieces of wax paper and gently pound until almost double in size. Sprinkle with 1/8 teaspoon of the salt and 1/16 teaspoon of the pepper.
2. In a large frying pan, heat olive oil over medium temperature. Add garlic and cook for 30 seconds, stirring. Add spinach and sauté until barely wilted; season with remaining salt and pepper.
3. Remove mixture from pan, let cool, and divide into 8 parts. Place one part of the spinach mixture on each chicken breast, sprinkle cheese evenly over chicken, roll up, and secure with toothpicks.
4. In a medium bowl, mix eggs and milk. In another shallow bowl, place bread crumbs. Dip chicken in egg mixture, then coat with bread crumbs.
5. Heat peanut oil in the frying pan over medium temperature. Add chicken and cook until browned. Transfer chicken to a baking dish covered with foil and bake at 350°F for about 30 minutes or until easy to pierce with a fork.
6. Strain most oil from pan, leaving brown bits on bottom. Add butter and melt over medium heat. Add mushrooms and sauté for 2 minutes. Stir in flour, cooking until thickened.
7. Mix chicken broth, wine, lemon juice, and capers into the sauce. Cook until thickened. Pour sauce over chicken rolls before serving.
